pr.title: Misc fixes to support compilation with GCC
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Small portability/build-fix PR for GCC compilation. It replaced an M2CTX-vs-runtime constant definition in fighter forward declarations with an enum constant, made a PowerPC SPR setup helper silently compile away under non-Metrowerks/GCC builds instead of hitting NOT_IMPLEMENTED, added a GCC-only empty __eabi stub called from main for EABI setup expectations, and guarded lbfile's old DVDFileInfo stack-size matching type so BUGFIX builds can use the real DVDFileInfo.
what_changed: In src/melee/ft/forward.h, Ft_Dynamics_NumMax changed from a conditional M2CTX macro/static ssize_t const to an anonymous enum constant with value 10.
In src/melee/gm/gmmain.c, init_spr_unk's remark was clarified to say it can safely be ignored when building with GCC.
In src/melee/gm/gmmain.c, the non-MWERKS_GEKKO branch no longer expands NOT_IMPLEMENTED inside init_spr_unk; the body is now only active under MWERKS_GEKKO.
In src/melee/gm/gmmain.c, a GCC-only empty void __eabi(void) stub was added with a comment that it is auto-called from main to set up the EABI environment.
In src/melee/lb/lbfile.c, OldDVDFileInfo is now only typedef'd when BUGFIX is not defined.
In src/melee/lb/lbfile.c, lbFile_8001634C uses DVDFileInfo under BUGFIX and OldDVDFileInfo otherwise, preserving the old stack-size matching behavior for non-BUGFIX builds.
smart_moves: Used an anonymous enum for Ft_Dynamics_NumMax, avoiding the need for ssize_t and avoiding conditional macro/static const differences between M2CTX and normal compilation.
Kept Metrowerks-specific SPR writes behind MWERKS_GEKKO while making GCC builds a no-op rather than a compile/runtime placeholder failure.
Added __eabi only under __GNUC__, limiting the stub to the toolchain that needs it and avoiding interference with the original matching compiler path.
Preserved the documented OldDVDFileInfo matching workaround for normal decomp builds while allowing BUGFIX builds to use the correct SDK DVDFileInfo layout.
decomp_lessons: For shared constants in headers, anonymous enum constants are often more portable than static typed const objects, especially when the type introduces host/toolchain dependencies such as ssize_t.
Hardware/compiler-specific initialization code should be isolated with precise preprocessor guards; a port compiler may need a safe no-op instead of NOT_IMPLEMENTED.
When supporting alternate compilers, provide minimal ABI hooks such as __eabi only under the relevant compiler guard rather than changing the original startup flow globally.
Bugfix/configuration builds may legitimately diverge from stack-size matching hacks; guard those divergences explicitly so the matching build remains stable.
naming_conventions: Ft_Dynamics_NumMax retained its existing CamelCase-like project constant spelling despite moving to an enum.
OldDVDFileInfo names a deliberate historical/layout compatibility struct distinct from the real DVDFileInfo.
Compiler/configuration guards used include M2CTX, MWERKS_GEKKO, __GNUC__, and BUGFIX.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replacing static ssize_t const with an enum constant avoids emitting storage and is usually safe for compile-time constants used in matching-sensitive code.
OldDVDFileInfo remains available in non-BUGFIX builds specifically to match stack allocation sizes, because the actual DVDFileInfo is documented as 4 bytes longer due to a callback field.
Metrowerks-only MTSPR writes in init_spr_unk remain guarded by MWERKS_GEKKO, keeping special register setup tied to the original target/compiler path.
The GCC __eabi stub satisfies compiler-generated startup expectations without adding behavior to the original Metrowerks path.
follow_up_queries: Look for later GCC support PRs that add more __GNUC__ guards or ABI stubs.
Search for other header constants using static typed const or M2CTX-only macros that could be converted to enum constants for portability.
Search for OldDVDFileInfo and DVDFileInfo BUGFIX usage to ensure matching-vs-correct-layout policy is consistent.
Audit NOT_IMPLEMENTED in code paths compiled by GCC or non-Metrowerks toolchains.
